user_query:
I’m organizing a small community meeting about Tamaizumi-ike and I need a grounded brief, a cleaned-up invite, and a simple meeting notes doc. Please work locally with the provided files and produce the outputs below.

Inputs to use:
- input/docs/tamaizumi_history.md
- input/docs/water_quality_report.txt
- input/data/past_events.csv
- input/draft_invite.txt
- scripts/validate_outputs.py (provided validator)

Deliverables:
1) A grounded fact set at output/facts/facts_summary.json. Create a JSON array of at least 5 facts pulled from the input files, each object with exactly these fields:
   - id: string like "F1", "F2", ... (unique)
   - fact_text: a concise factual statement (<=280 characters)
   - source_file: one of the provided input file paths you used
   - support_lines: [start, end] (1-indexed line numbers in source_file)
   - support_excerpt: the exact text from source_file for the specified support_lines
   Coverage requirements: include at least one numeric fact grounded in input/docs/water_quality_report.txt, at least one fact grounded in input/data/past_events.csv with a concrete event detail (e.g., date/participants/notes), and at least one qualitative detail grounded in input/docs/tamaizumi_history.md. The support_excerpt must be exact text from those lines.

2) A rewritten invitation at output/email_invite.txt. Rewrite input/draft_invite.txt to be warm, clear, and concise (<=1200 characters), preserving the date (2024-05-10), time (18:00), location (Community Center Room 2), and the purpose (community meeting about Tamaizumi-ike). Keep the key details while improving tone and brevity.

3) Meeting notes at output/meeting_notes.md with these sections: a title line "Tamaizumi-ike Community Meeting", a date line "Date: 2024-05-10", a section "Agenda", a section "Key facts" that lists the facts from output/facts/facts_summary.json with their ids, a section "Decisions" (you may leave items as TBD), and a section "Action items" with at least 3 actionable bullets. Each action item must reference a fact by including its id in square brackets, e.g., "[F2]".

4) Run the validator and save its output to output/validation/validation_report.txt:
   - Command: python3 scripts/validate_outputs.py > output/validation/validation_report.txt

The task is complete when the validator reports success. Do not use the internet; ground everything strictly in the provided input files. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
